profile.name: Abhiram Bondada
profile.title: Software Development Engineer at Microsoft
profile.bio: I am a software engineer with a passion for building scalable and efficient systems. I am currently working as a software engineer at Microsoft.
profile.availability: Collaboration projects, Freelance work, and Content creation.
profile.tagline: Building scalable systems and AI-powered solutions

achievements: Achieved ~99.9% availability and solved global outages in Authentication API traffic by architecting a Bulkhead isolation strategy with intelligent traffic partitioning
Reduced p99 latency by ~75% using Bloom filters for request routing and scale-group caches for improved cache hit rates

Saved $9.2M+ annually in cloud costs by identifying systemic inefficiencies in Azure engineering spend through stale-resource detection, automated cleanup, and a developer inner loop for infrastructure management
Led development of AI-powered On-call Engineer Assistant to automate signal correlation and context building, reducing incident triage time from ~1 day to near-real time

Enabled 2000+ customers to adopt Microsoft Sentinel (SIEM+SOAR) by building Dynamics 365 audit telemetry pipelines, onboarding 5+ security partners via a webhook in Power Platform Admin Center, and contributing 15+ production-grade threat-hunting queries to Microsoft open source

experience.description: Product management rotation focused on Microsoft Fabric adoption strategy and customer data consumption experiences for enterprise energy customers.
achievements: Developed Microsoft Fabric adoption strategy for Energy vertical, creating roadmap for customer migration
Authored product specifications for Customer Data Consumption experiences improving data accessibility
Conducted customer discovery sessions with Equinor, Halliburton, and Schlumberger to gather requirements
Collaborated with engineering teams to translate customer needs into actionable product features
Tripled ingestion throughput (7GB → 20GB file sizes) by re-architecting the platform from managed to self-hosted Apache Airflow and tuning Azure Kubernetes CPU resource tiers
Mitigated ~150 security vulnerabilities (CVEs), reduced container image sizes by ~80%, and build time by ~10% using multi-stage Docker files and Distroless Azure Linux base images

Accelerated development cycles by ~90% for partners by replacing manual workflows with a secure cross-tenant CI/CD automation, shortening approval timelines from 2 months to 1 week with zero manual interventions
Enabled consistent post-release API documentation updates for 25+ service/partner teams, saving ~1 developer-day per service per release by integrating automated Swagger spec publishing into CI/CD
Onboarded Schlumberger Data workspace on Azure PaaS which enabled timely release of SLB's first revenue generating Azure solution
Reduced infrastructure spend by ~$25K per month by evaluating Redis-based caching for storage-heavy workloads and rejecting the design after benchmarking showed low reuse and poor ROI

experience.description: Research assistant under Prof. Praveen Paruchuri at the Machine Learning Lab, focusing on Algorithmic Game Theory and its applications in security and behavioral modeling.
achievements: Researched bounded rationality models in security games, exploring human decision-making patterns
Developed simulation frameworks for evaluating game-theoretic security strategies in Bayesian Stackelberg games

honors: Teaching Assistant for Probability and Linear Algebra courses (2020 – 2021)
Student Researcher in Machine Learning, Security, Algorithmic Game Theory, under Advisor: Dr. Praveen Paruchuri

certifications.name: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate
Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Scientist Associate
